skill.name: seo-content-refresher
skill.description: Reviews existing SEO content for outdated dates, statistics, examples, terminology, and missing recent developments.
skill.summary: Finds content refresh opportunities and turns them into prioritized SEO update plans.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and all are false positives. Line 3 is frontmatter text, line 25 is a Markdown resource reference, and lines 76-85 are a fenced output example. No executable code, command invocation, weak cryptography, data exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3, but the line is YAML description text for the skill. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto usage is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains only the frontmatter description. There is no code or cryptographic reference to evaluate as unsafe.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Resource Reference
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 25 as shell backtick execution. The line uses Markdown inline code to name resources/implementation-playbook.md, not a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown formatting around a relative resource path. No command, interpreter, or user input execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Fenced Output Example
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md lines 76-85 as shell backtick execution. These lines are a Markdown fenced example of the expected content refresh plan format.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited block is a documentation example under the Output section. It contains no shell syntax, executable instruction, or dynamic input handling.

content.value_statement: Old statistics, dated examples, and stale terminology can weaken search performance and user trust. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code find refresh opportunities and create prioritized update plans.
actual_capabilities: Identifies dates, statistics, examples, and references that may need updates.
Prioritizes pages by ranking loss, traffic decline, seasonality, and information age.
Suggests refresh actions such as updated data, new sections, and current examples.
Recommends freshness signals including modified dates, internal links, and schema updates.
Creates page-level refresh plans with priority, actions, and deliverables.
limitations: Does not browse the web or verify current facts unless the host assistant has browsing tools.
Requires the user to provide page content, URLs, metrics, or business context.
Does not replace a full SEO platform, analytics tool, or human editorial review.
Cannot guarantee ranking improvements after content updates.

use_cases.title: Update Declining Blog Posts
use_cases.description: Find outdated claims and missing developments in older articles before an editorial refresh.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Refresh Backlogs
use_cases.description: Sort many candidate pages into high, medium, and low update priorities.

use_cases.title: Prepare Client Refresh Plans
use_cases.description: Turn audit notes into practical update recommendations for client review.

prompt_templates.title: Find Stale Elements
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this article for outdated dates, statistics, examples, and terminology. List each issue and suggest a practical update.

prompt_templates.title: Create Refresh Priority
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess these pages for content freshness. Use traffic, ranking change, seasonality, and information age to assign high, medium, or low priority.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Editorial Update Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a content refresh brief for this page. Include target sections, data to replace, examples to update, and new sections to add.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Refresh Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a quarterly SEO content refresh program for this site. Include selection criteria, review steps, publishing cadence, and success metrics.

output_examples.input: An article last updated in 2022 with old survey data and examples from 2020.
output: Priority: High because the article depends on time-sensitive data.
Refresh actions: replace old statistics, add current examples, update title year, and add a recent trends section.
Verification: check sources, update modified date, and review internal links.

output_examples.input: A list of ten evergreen pages with mixed traffic and ranking movement.
output: Group pages by ranking decline, traffic value, and visible outdated information.
Schedule high-priority pages first, then review stagnant pages during the monthly content cycle.
Track refreshed pages with publish date, action owner, and post-update performance.

best_practices: Provide page content, last updated dates, target keywords, and performance metrics when available.
Ask for specific refresh actions, not only a general quality review.
Verify new statistics and examples with current authoritative sources before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not change dates only to appear fresh without improving the content.
Do not refresh every page with the same template or section structure.
Do not remove useful historical context when it still supports the reader.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews existing content for outdated statistics, dates, examples, terminology, links, and missing recent developments.

faq.question: Can it verify current facts?
faq.answer: It can suggest what to verify. Fact verification depends on whether the host assistant has access to current sources.

faq.question: Does it write the refreshed article?
faq.answer: It focuses on refresh plans and update recommendations. You can ask the assistant to draft revisions afterward.

faq.question: Which content works best with this skill?
faq.answer: It works best on SEO articles, landing pages, guides, comparison pages, and seasonal content with visible dates or data.

faq.question: How should I measure results?
faq.answer: Track rankings, organic traffic, click-through rate, engagement, conversions, and indexation after publishing updates.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation only, with no executable commands, network access, or prompt injection evidence.
